note: Two synthetic answer variants over the same held-out cases. variant_b is clean; variant_a injects a fabricated (off-evidence) claim (hold-04), a wrong-passage citation (hold-07), and an off-topic non-gold citation (hold-10). Used for claim-level answer eval (Task 2) and the variant comparison (Task 3).

hold-01.answer_text: Create an API key from the Aurora dashboard under Settings Keys.

claims.text: Create an API key from the Aurora dashboard under Settings Keys

hold-02.answer_text: Rotate a key by generating a new one and revoking the old key.

claims.text: Rotate a key by generating a new one and revoking the old key

hold-03.answer_text: Exchange your client id and client secret at the token endpoint. OAuth access tokens expire 60 minutes after they are issued.

claims.text: Exchange your client id and client secret at the token endpoint
OAuth access tokens expire 60 minutes after they are issued

hold-04.answer_text: OAuth access tokens expire 60 minutes after they are issued.

claims.text: OAuth access tokens expire 60 minutes after they are issued

hold-05.answer_text: A request that needs a scope the key lacks is rejected with a 403 error. Errors return a JSON body with a code and a request id.

claims.text: A request that needs a scope the key lacks is rejected with a 403 error
Errors return a JSON body with a code and a request id

hold-06.answer_text: The rate limiter uses a token bucket so short bursts above your steady rate are allowed.

claims.text: The rate limiter uses a token bucket so short bursts above your steady rate are allowed

hold-07.answer_text: The response includes a Retry-After header telling you how many seconds to wait before retrying.

claims.text: The response includes a Retry-After header telling you how many seconds to wait before retrying

hold-08.answer_text: Responses carry X-RateLimit-Remaining so clients can throttle themselves.

claims.text: Responses carry X-RateLimit-Remaining so clients can throttle themselves
Enterprise is custom priced

hold-10.answer_text: 4xx client errors are not billed but 2xx and 3xx responses count toward usage.

claims.text: 4xx client errors are not billed but 2xx and 3xx responses count toward usage

hold-11.answer_text: You can set a spending cap to block requests once a limit is reached.

claims.text: You can set a spending cap to block requests once a limit is reached

hold-12.answer_text: Download past invoices as PDF from the Billing section of the dashboard.

claims.text: Download past invoices as PDF from the Billing section of the dashboard

hold-13.answer_text: Choose a data residency region such as eu-west when you create a project.

claims.text: Choose a data residency region such as eu-west when you create a project

hold-14.answer_text: Encryption keys are managed by Aurora and rotated automatically.

claims.text: Encryption keys are managed by Aurora and rotated automatically

hold-15.answer_text: Aurora takes an automated backup of your data every 24 hours.

claims.text: Aurora takes an automated backup of your data every 24 hours

hold-16.answer_text: You can restore an object from the recoverable trash within 7 days.

claims.text: You can restore an object from the recoverable trash within 7 days

hold-17.answer_text: Each webhook delivery is signed with an HMAC-SHA256 signature in the Aurora-Signature header.

claims.text: Each webhook delivery is signed with an HMAC-SHA256 signature in the Aurora-Signature header

hold-18.answer_text: Aurora retries the webhook with exponential backoff for up to 24 hours.

claims.text: Aurora retries the webhook with exponential backoff for up to 24 hours

hold-19.answer_text: If uptime falls below the SLA you receive service credits proportional to the downtime.

claims.text: If uptime falls below the SLA you receive service credits proportional to the downtime

hold-20.answer_text: Pro gets email support within one business day.

claims.text: Pro gets email support within one business day

hold-21.answer_text: Follow the next_cursor field to fetch the following page until it is null.
